name: SCATTER

meta.data_required: entities with two numeric measures; under ~20 points if labeled

use_when: how two measures relate across entities
finding the outlier (the point the title talks about)
not_when: time is one of the axes (use CH-TIM-01)
hundreds of points (labels collapse; use CH-COR-03)

data_shape.title: string, action title naming the outlier or the relationship with numbers
data_shape.eyebrow: string caps, rendered only when the DL chrome asks for it
data_shape.unit_x: string appended to the max x tick
data_shape.unit_y: string appended to the max y tick
data_shape.points: array of {label, x >= 0, y >= 0, highlight?}, one highlight max

scale.algorithm: both axes: FN-05 nice-scale from each axis max

rules: both axes start at zero; both use the FN-05 tick algorithm
every point labeled directly (this entry caps at ~20 points, so labels fit)
one highlight maximum: role primary with ink label; the rest muted
gridlines both directions, hairline zero lines
units once per axis, on the max tick
anti_patterns: trend lines without stating the fit (no silent regressions)
point clouds too dense to label (wrong entry)
qa: both axes at zero with algorithmic ticks
title names the outlier or relationship with numbers
